concept: GVEA's Turbine Ticket: a proud North Pole power plant holds his place in a global, AI-driven turbine backlog line, and the neighbor breathing the haze his hedge would create is the honest, unresolved cost.
treatment: Winning treatment: THE ENTHUSIAST ("The Ticket in the Cold"), scored by the writers-room judge over Comedian/Dramatist/Explainer. Hero Sourdough (net-new personified North Pole Power Plant, Character-base + custom glow chest) plans ahead, paying $50,000 to hold a turbine order slot amid a global AI-data-center-driven backlog, hedging with battery sidekick Cell. Judge reasoning: the locked story is a hopeful/competence beat and the routine needed to break a 3-run cautionary skew; Enthusiast was the only treatment scoring top on BOTH fairness (competence AND the PM2.5 counterpoint genuinely land) and freshness. Grafted beats: from the Dramatist, the parallel GE Vernova/Siemens Energy queues, the 2024/2025 supply-loss escalation, and the freeze-mid-decision/loopback button (guarantees the honest open question so a hopeful winner never collapses into a booster ending); from the Comedian, the Moose-bumped-from-the-dispenser running gag paying off with '#4,000,001 - DATA CENTERS AHEAD OF YOU'; from the Explainer, the $50K-deposit-vs-$90-120M-bill scale contrast and the GE Vernova/Siemens Energy stat badges, built from the HUD kit rather than a costly new conveyor mechanism. Sourdough's arc: earned confidence -> provisional humility. He does not resolve to triumph or defeat; the board vote stays genuinely open through the final freeze.
angle_ref: out/dispatch/angle.json (Phase 3.5): thesis = a global AI-driven turbine market is setting terms inside an Interior Alaska boardroom; GVEA is so far acting like a sophisticated buyer, not a victim, but the competence is provisional until the July 28 vote and the PM2.5 non-attainment cost are honestly shown. Stance: curious with earned, provisional competence.
divergence_note: Diverges from the last 2 dispatches on 6-7 of 7 controlled axes and both freetext axes. 2026-07-17 (UAF critical minerals) was a cross-section-cutaway/vertical-descent/stacked-layers editorial-schematic drilling into bedrock; this board is eye-level-immersive/push-in-only/centered-hero-lowerthird naturalistic-scene, a character portrait, not a cutaway. 2026-07-18 (AIDEA Mat-Su) was an aerial-to-ground faceless-institution-monolith with a loopback-vigil layout and dawn-backlight; this board's hero is a warm, expressive SINGLE character (Sourdough) rather than a faceless monolith, its light story is cold-dawn-to-haze-red rather than dawn-backlight, and its central metaphor (a personified plant holding a place in a literal deli-style global order queue) is unrelated to either prior episode's drill or land-conveyance metaphors. Palette (cold indigo pre-sunrise + ember-orange + server-teal + ash-red) shares no tokens with 07-17's amethyst-plum/copper/citrine/phosphor-lime or 07-18's dawn/birch/viridian/husky-amber/graphite/crimson.

fingerprint.palette: cold indigo pre-sunrise sky, ember-orange furnace glow, server-teal queue light, ash-red haze warning
fingerprint.metaphor: a proud local power plant holds his place in a global turbine backlog line, and a neighbor at the fence breathes what that line costs

hook.frame1: Sourdough's mittened hand holds a frost-crusted $50,000 ticket stub up against a cold indigo pre-sunrise sky, his furnace-window chest glowing warm behind it.
hook.headline: He Paid $50K Just To Wait
hook.loopback: The frozen ticket stub from frame 1 becomes the frozen, unsigned ballot in the final freeze, same mittened hand, same held pose.

shots.centered: Sourdough pinning his frost-crusted $50,000 ticket to his glowing chest
shots.means: opens on the stake: a paid place in line

hero.name: Sourdough
hero.detail_note: frost-rimmed rivets on a stubby turbine-hall body, a warm furnace-window chest, a knit watch-cap, mittened hands pinning the ticket

shots.centered: the backlog counter ticking up behind Sourdough, tiny at the head of a global line
shots.means: scale reveal: he is the smallest customer in the biggest auction on Earth

hero.name: The Order Queue
hero.detail_note: a ticket dispenser, a conveyor of riveted turbine crates, a spinning GW backlog counter, blue data-center slices flipping down the line

shots.centered: the GE Vernova and Siemens Energy stat badges stamping in over the queue
shots.means: names the antagonist force in hard numbers, with a Moose gag to keep it human

hero.name: Backlog Stat Panel
hero.detail_note: a GE Vernova StatBurst badge, a Siemens Energy nameplate, a bumped-Moose gag vignette in the corner

shots.centered: the old and new turbines standing side by side as the price-range ruler stretches out
shots.means: the competence beat: he already moved, at a real, honestly-ranged cost

hero.name: North Pole Power Plant (old + new turbine)
hero.detail_note: the veteran 2006 LM6000 unit, the new ~45MW turbine slotting in, two SwingSigns (Southcentral/Enstar) swinging shut, a stretching price-range ruler

shots.centered: the red PM2.5 haze grid settling over North Pole as Patrice Lee's verbatim quote types on screen
shots.means: THE TURN: the honest counterpoint lands as an undodged picture

hero.name: North Pole under the haze
hero.detail_note: a translucent red PM2.5 grid overlay, Patrice Lee's typed verbatim quote card, a small Delta Wind SurveyStake spinning

shots.centered: Sourdough frozen mid-vote, pen hovering over an unsigned ballot
shots.means: THE BUTTON: an honest freeze that loops back to the ticket motif; the Moose gag pays off

hero.name: Sourdough (frozen)
hero.detail_note: an unsigned ballot under a hovering pen, the Moose's disbelieving face at the dispenser reading #4,000,001, the ticket motif rhyme

beats.vo: A power plant in North Pole is holding a ticket.
beats.shows: Sourdough pins a frost-crusted $50,000 ticket to his glowing chest
beats.means: cold open: the stake is a paid place in line

draw.subject: Sourdough, a warm glowing-chested power-plant character
draw.action: blows frost off a paper ticket and pins it to his chest

choreo.ambient: drifting frost motes and breath fog

beats.vo: Fifty thousand dollars, just to keep his place in line.
beats.shows: camera pulls back revealing a global order queue stretching to the horizon
beats.means: scale reveal: he is tiny in an enormous line

draw.subject: the global order queue
draw.action: recedes to a vanishing point behind Sourdough as a backlog counter spins up

beats.vo: The line is a global turbine backlog, jammed by AI data centers.
beats.shows: glowing blue data-center silhouettes cut into the line ahead of him
beats.means: names the antagonist force: global AI-driven demand

draw.subject: data-center silhouettes
draw.action: slide ahead of Sourdough in the queue, tickets already stamped SOLD

choreo.reaction: Sourdough leans to keep his spot

beats.vo: GE Vernova's backlog: near a hundred gigawatts.
beats.shows: a GE Vernova StatBurst badge stamps in with the backlog counter spinning up
beats.means: the scale of the global backlog, made numeric

draw.subject: a StatBurst badge
draw.action: stamps onto the queue as the backlog counter spins to a huge number

beats.vo: A fifth of it, data centers.
beats.shows: a background Moose gets bumped from the ticket dispenser by a data-center hand
beats.means: comic beat dramatizing the AI-share of the backlog

draw.subject: a background Moose
draw.action: reaches for a ticket and gets shoved back by a data-center hand

beats.vo: Siemens Energy: over sixty percent of this year's orders are data centers.
beats.shows: a Siemens Energy nameplate lights up beside a second queue mostly flipped blue
beats.means: a second, starker data point on AI's share of turbine demand

draw.subject: a Siemens Energy nameplate
draw.action: lights up as a parallel queue's tickets flip server-blue one by one

beats.vo: Fairbanks lost its cheap power in twenty twenty four,
beats.shows: a SwingSign labeled Cheap Southcentral Power swings shut on the AlaskaMini map
beats.means: establishes why the co-op needs new capacity at all, independent of AI

draw.subject: a SwingSign on the AlaskaMini map
draw.action: swings shut over the Southcentral supply line

beats.vo: its gas deal in twenty twenty five.
beats.shows: a second supply pipe labeled Enstar cracks and snaps shut
beats.means: the second door closing, hardening the plant's resolve

draw.subject: a supply pipe labeled Enstar
draw.action: cracks and snaps shut on the map

beats.vo: The plant planned ahead: a second turbine, ninety to one hundred twenty million dollars, plus a battery bet.
beats.shows: a new ~45MW turbine slots in beside the veteran 2006 unit as a price-range ruler stretches out and Cell's charge bars glow
beats.means: the competence beat: he already moved, at a real, honestly-ranged cost

draw.subject: Sourdough and battery sidekick Cell
draw.action: stand together as a new turbine slots in beside the old one and a price ruler stretches out

choreo.reaction: Cell's charge bars pop up with a spring overshoot

beats.vo: But North Pole already fails the federal air test.
beats.shows: camera pushes past Sourdough's glowing chest and tilts up as a translucent red haze grid settles over the town
beats.means: THE TURN: the honest counterpoint arrives as a picture

draw.subject: the ground and air over North Pole
draw.action: a translucent red PM2.5 haze grid settles over the rooftops as the camera tilts up

choreo.reaction: Sourdough's glow dims a shade and he lowers a mask from his chest

beats.vo: "You cannot add to the pollution in a non-attainment area, and be legally correct."
beats.shows: Patrice Lee stands at the fence line as her exact words type out on screen
beats.sfx: typewriter-tick + a real dip in the music
beats.means: the fair counterpoint lands verbatim, from a named person

draw.subject: Patrice Lee, a neighbor character at the fence line
draw.action: stands in the haze as her verbatim quote types out beside her

beats.shows: a SurveyStake plants quietly beside Sourdough with a small spinning wind-turbine icon
beats.means: the honest, unresolved alternative, offered not crowned

draw.subject: a SurveyStake
draw.action: plants in the frost beside Sourdough, a small wind turbine spinning atop it

beats.vo: The board votes July twenty eighth.
beats.shows: a GearLever labeled BOARD VOTE hovers between YES and NO as a wall clock ticks
beats.means: the concrete, dated stake of the still-open decision

draw.subject: a GearLever labeled BOARD VOTE
draw.action: hovers between YES and NO as a wall clock behind it ticks toward the date

beats.shows: Sourdough freezes mid-vote with a pen hovering over an unsigned ballot, while the Moose finally reaches the dispenser and reads his own absurd number in disbelief
beats.sfx: held silence then a soft final chime, the ticket motif returns
beats.means: THE BUTTON: an honest freeze, looping back to the ticket motif; the Moose gag pays off

draw.subject: Sourdough
draw.action: freezes mid-vote, pen hovering over an unsigned ballot
draw.annotation: #4,000,001 - DATA CENTERS AHEAD OF YOU (Moose's ticket, background)

choreo.reaction: Moose's disbelieving double-take in the background
